topic: King's Chamber 117 Hz — re-review triggered by 67% drift in detail_verdict_length after FLAG removal (ISS-1070 fix)
change_summary: detail_verdict_length shrank from 849 → 280 chars (-67%), driven by removal of the EXP-054 FLAG paragraph via ISS-1070 (fixed 2026-04-17). All 5 head candidates in drift-audit.json (generated 2026-05-28T01:09Z, 72h stale) were OBE (re-reviewed since audit ran); fell through to §1f in-prompt scan fallback; obe_skipped=5 (WIN-006/004/029/066/023). WIN-003 emerged as top non-OBE candidate by max drift (67% on detail_verdict_length).
our_argument_summary: WIN-003 Misleading stands. 117 Hz is acoustic resonance of the King's Chamber sarcophagus (Reid 1996–97); 10×7.83 = 78.3 Hz fails the Schumann harmonic claim by 33%; the 117 Hz = 10 × 11.7 Hz coincidence to WIN-001's Tesla number has no physical mechanism (EM-to-acoustic coupling unaddressed); monitor.py has no domain — the value is hardcoded metadata. The FLAG-removal patch (ISS-1070) cleanly resolved the open carve-out boilerplate, but the c2-pq4 review's H1 (granite-speed-of-sound scientific error in detail_evidence) and H3 (15th harmonic numerical gap, 7.83 × 15 ≈ 117.45 Hz, much closer than the 10th harmonic our text checks) both remain unaddressed in current text.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No domain in monitor.py for WIN-003. Value 117.0 Hz exists only as hardcoded metadata in inject_ai_layer.py: {id: WIN-003, observed: 117.0 Hz, status: confirmed}. No formula, no comparison, no API.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Pure display — the 'WIN' is metadata-asserted, not computed or validated.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — there is nothing to escape; no test exists.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py domain for WIN-003. inject_ai_layer.py contains the value as static metadata. Confirms c2-pq4 finding; current text already states this clearly: 'WIN-003 has no domain test in monitor.py'.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: 117 Hz is a well-known acoustic eigenmode (Reid 1996–97). The dome attaches the label '10th harmonic of dome resonance' to an existing standard-physics measurement (Helmholtz / room acoustics) — that IS relabeling. Note: c2-pq4 marked this false on the grounds that the dome 'fabricates a connection' rather than 'renames a cause.' Re-examining: the underlying observation (117 Hz sarcophagus resonance) is fully explained by air-cavity acoustics; the dome's contribution is renaming that mechanism as 'dome harmonic structure.' That fits the 'relabels_standard' definition more cleanly than 'fabricates novel claim,' since the 117 Hz number itself is standard, only the explanatory mechanism is being substituted.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Reid's 117 Hz measurement was published ~1997 on cymascope.com and widely circulated in alt-science communities. Dome model adopted the known value as a 'prediction' decades later. The carve-out review (c2-pq4) confirmed all three legitimate-novelty criteria fail (no derivation from dome parameters, value publicly indexed, no derivation published with value).
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: 117 Hz is not derivable from dome parameters. Disc radius 20,015 km → c/(2D) ≈ 7.5 Hz; firmament height 9,086 km → c/(2H) ≈ 16.5 Hz; no combination of dome geometry constants yields 117 Hz. Value comes from acoustic measurement of a stone cavity.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER FROM c2-pq4 H1, STILL OPEN: detail_evidence still asserts 'acoustic eigenmodes determined by chamber/sarcophagus geometry and the speed of sound in granite (~5,800–6,000 m/s) — standard Helmholtz resonance.' This is physically wrong. The 117 Hz resonance is an AIR-cavity eigenmode of the sarcophagus interior, not a granite-wall standing-wave mode. Speed of sound in granite (5,900 m/s) applied to the 2.3 m sarcophagus length gives v/(2L) ≈ 1,283 Hz for the half-wave fundamental (an order of magnitude too high), or v/(4L) ≈ 641 Hz for the closed-end quarter-wave (also far too high). Speed of sound in air (343 m/s) gives v/(2L) ≈ 74.6 Hz fundamental and predicts 117 Hz as a higher-order air-cavity mode (wavelength 2.93 m, consistent with combined sarcophagus dimensions ~2.27 × 0.97 × 0.87 m). The granite walls supply rigid acoustic boundary conditions; they do NOT set the resonant frequency. A dome defender who knows basic room acoustics can quote our text back at us: 'your debunk doesn't even understand which medium is resonating — why should we trust the EM analysis?' The core argument (acoustic ≠ electromagnetic) still holds, but this specific physics claim must be corrected. No open ISS exists for this hole (decider should create one).
holes_found.recommendation: Edit detail_evidence: replace 'the speed of sound in granite (~5,800–6,000 m/s) — standard Helmholtz resonance' with text identifying the resonance as an air-cavity acoustic mode bounded by the granite walls, with the speed of sound in air (~343 m/s) setting the frequency scale; note that the granite (~5,900 m/s) would give frequencies ~1,283 Hz for the same chamber, confirming the resonating medium is the enclosed air not the stone.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER FROM c2-pq4 H3, STILL OPEN: current detail_evidence checks only 10 × 7.83 = 78.3 Hz against the 117 Hz observation (a 33% miss). A dome defender could shift to '15th harmonic of Schumann' since 15 × 7.83 = 117.45 Hz (0.4% off) — much closer than our text's chosen comparator. Acknowledging and dismissing this strengthens the text. The dismissal is straightforward: arbitrary integer multiples of 7.83 will hit any target band ±4 Hz purely by counting (Schumann harmonics are spaced 7.83 Hz, so within any 8 Hz window there is always at least one), so 15 × 7.83 ≈ 117.45 is numerology, not prediction; the dome model doesn't pre-specify which harmonic n to test, so post-hoc selection of n=15 is a researcher-degrees-of-freedom abuse. No open ISS exists (decider should create one).
holes_found.recommendation: Append to detail_evidence after the 10 × 7.83 line: 'Even if the dome retreats to a different harmonic — 15 × 7.83 = 117.45 Hz (≠ 117.0 Hz, but 0.4% close) — the claim would still be numerology without a mechanism. The Schumann harmonics are spaced 7.83 Hz, so any acoustic frequency below ~150 Hz will sit within 4 Hz of some integer multiple by counting alone. The model does not pre-specify which harmonic n must match; post-hoc selection of n=15 is researcher-degrees-of-freedom abuse, not prediction.'

holes_found.description: INTERNAL TENSION: code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard is currently false in data/wins.json's code_analysis (and matched the c2-pq4 review's classification on grounds that the dome 'fabricates a connection' rather than 'renames a cause'). Re-examining under the published definition in this prompt: 'The phenomenon this WIN claims is already predicted and explained by standard physics … The dome model's contribution is renaming the mechanism.' 117 Hz IS predicted and explained by standard room/cavity acoustics (Reid's documented eigenmode). The dome contributes the rename 'dome harmonic' for that mechanism. This appears to fit relabels_standard=true better than =false. Setting aside re-classification, the more important point is that the tag and the rebuttal text disagree: detail_evidence's strongest line ('acoustic ≠ electromagnetic — different physics') only works if there IS a standard-physics explanation we're contrasting against. Either flip the tag to true (recommended) and tighten the prose accordingly, or articulate why 'fabricating a connection between two real phenomena' is not relabeling in the same way 'renaming an existing cause' is. The current ambiguity is exploitable: a defender can point to relabels_standard=false and say 'even your own metadata admits this isn't just relabeled standard physics.'
holes_found.recommendation: Flip code_analysis.relabels_standard from false to true in data/wins.json. Update code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence accordingly. If Part 2b prose references aggregate relabels_standard counts (e.g., '14/31 relabel standard physics'), recompute. Alternatively, document the tag-rule edge case ('rename mechanism' vs 'fabricate connection between two real phenomena') in the methodology so the choice is principled.

stronger_arguments.description: Air-cavity calculation strengthens the acoustic-vs-EM contrast quantitatively. At 117 Hz, λ_air = 2.93 m, comparable to combined sarcophagus dimensions; the resonance is a coupled-mode standing wave in the enclosed air. Standard cavity acoustics predicts this WITHOUT any EM theory; the dome model adds nothing. Stating this calculation explicitly preempts the 'you don't even know what's resonating' attack.
sources: Speed of sound in air at 20°C ≈ 343 m/s; v/2L for L=2.27m gives f_1 ≈ 75.6 Hz; (2,0,0) longitudinal mode at 151 Hz; 117 Hz consistent with mixed-axis higher-order air mode. Standard reference: Kinsler et al., Fundamentals of Acoustics, ch. 9.

stronger_arguments.description: The 'Tesla 11.79 Hz × 10 = 117.9 Hz / 11.7 Hz × 10 = 117 Hz' coincidence in current text leans on WIN-001 (Tesla resonance, currently 'Refuted by Data'). If the dome's Tesla claim is itself refuted (no derivation, formula attributed to Tesla actually requires globe diameter), then 117 = 10 × 11.7 isn't a 'coincidence with the dome's Tesla number' — it's a coincidence with a number the dome shouldn't be using in the first place. Cross-citing WIN-001's refutation explicitly tightens this rebuttal.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome's instinct that the King's Chamber 117 Hz measurement is unusual and worth attention has merit (data/wins.json:003 detail_evidence). Reid's cymatics work (https://www.cymascope.com/cyma_research/archaeoacoustics.html) is a real measurement of a real eigenmode in a real chamber — the resonance is not fabricated. Acoustic eigenmodes of ancient stone chambers are a legitimate niche topic in archaeoacoustics, and 117 Hz IS measured at the sarcophagus.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Acknowledging the measurement is real (WIN-003 detail_evidence) doesn't validate the dome's interpretation. The mechanism is air-cavity acoustics, fully predicted by standard room-mode theory; the harmonic-of-dome-resonance framing has (i) no specified base frequency that yields 117 Hz from dome geometry (data/wins.json:003 detail_verdict_text), (ii) no mechanism connecting EM dome modes to mechanical stone vibration, and (iii) post-hoc harmonic selection (10th vs 15th) — see Hole #2. The dome model takes credit for predicting Reid's pre-existing measurement; that's the post_hoc problem this WIN already documents.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Acoustic vs electromagnetic is a clean intuition, but Tesla himself wrote about piezoelectric coupling in quartz and stress-induced electromagnetic emission from compressed stone (e.g., earthquake-light phenomena, Brady & Rowell 1986). The King's Chamber is granite (quartz-bearing). If acoustic vibration at 117 Hz mechanically stresses the granite, the dome model could claim it would in principle drive a faint piezoelectric EM signal at the same frequency, providing the missing mechanism the debunk says is absent. The debunk dismisses 'no mechanism' too quickly — there is a textbook piezoelectric/triboelectric pathway from sound to weak EM, even if the magnitude is negligible.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Optional one-sentence note in detail_evidence after the mechanism paragraph: 'Piezoelectric coupling in quartz-bearing granite is a real but vanishingly weak effect (electric fields ~µV/m at audio drive levels); even charitably granting such a pathway, the dome model neither computes the expected EM signal nor measures it, so the mechanism remains unsubstantiated rather than absent.' This concedes the kernel of truth in the strongest defense while showing it doesn't get the dome over the bar.

cross_win_consistency.notes: WIN-001 (Tesla 11.78 Hz, verdict Refuted by Data — no dome derivation, formula needs globe diameter) is the source of the '10 × 11.7 = 117' coincidence in WIN-003 detail_evidence; using WIN-001's number here is fine because WIN-003 explicitly calls it a coincidence with no mechanism. WIN-002 (Schumann 7.83 Hz, Self-Contradicted) supports the '10 × 7.83 = 78.3 Hz fails' rebuttal directly. WIN-015 (Meyl scalar Faraday, Not Demonstrated) is the cousin claim that 'acoustic stone resonance somehow couples to EM' — both WINs share the unargued-mechanism problem and reinforce each other rhetorically. No contradictions across the four WIN texts.

claims_checked: 15 × 7.83 Hz = 117.45 Hz (missing from text — defender's likely rebuttal)
117 Hz vs speed of sound in granite vs air
quantitative_verification.computations: 10 × 7.83 = 78.30 ✓ (matches text). 15 × 7.83 = 117.45 ✓ (much closer to 117 than 10th harmonic, currently unaddressed). v_granite/(2L) at L=2.3m = 5900/4.6 = 1282.6 Hz (would-be granite fundamental, an order of magnitude too high). v_air/(2L) at L=2.3m = 343/4.6 = 74.6 Hz (air fundamental); 117 Hz wavelength in air = 2.93 m (plausible for higher-order sarcophagus air mode). Tesla 11.79 × 10 = 117.9 Hz; Tesla 11.7 × 10 = 117.0 Hz.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: The text's implied attribution of the 117 Hz acoustic eigenmode to speed of sound in granite is dimensionally wrong: granite-speed-of-sound would predict resonances 5–17x higher than the observation. The math forces the resonating medium to be air. See Hole #1.

citations_verified: Reid cymatics work / 117 Hz sarcophagus (cymascope.com archive, ~1997)
citations_unchecked: Specific dimensions of King's Chamber sarcophagus (cited generally; no dimensions explicitly given in current text)

deeper_analysis: The c2-pq4 review (2026-04-10) flagged three holes: H1 (granite-speed-of-sound scientific error — moderate), H2 (FLAG paragraph needs resolution — moderate), H3 (15th harmonic gap — minor). H2 was fixed via ISS-1070 (decider self-apply, 2026-04-17), which removed the EXP-054 carve-out FLAG and produced the 67% drift on detail_verdict_length that triggered this re-review. H1 and H3 never produced open issues and remain in the current text 7 weeks after they were first flagged. The granite/air error is the most exploitable for a defender — it's a basic acoustics error in prose that claims acoustic expertise. The 15th-harmonic gap is rhetorically minor but cheap to close. Additionally, the relabels_standard=false tag (set in c2-pq4) doesn't survive re-examination under the published tag definition; flipping it to true is cleaner and tightens the rebuttal logic.
summary_for_decider: Two unfixed carry-over holes from c2-pq4 (granite-speed-of-sound moderate H1; 15th-harmonic minor H3) plus one new moderate finding (relabels_standard tag conflicts with rebuttal logic). No matching open-issues exist for the carry-overs — decider should create them. Recommended action: minor_edit. No verdict change. Confidence high (0.92) that Misleading verdict stands. Anti-staleness note: the c2-pq4 review is 51 days old; this is the first deep re-review of WIN-003 since the FLAG fix landed.
